dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, {quest_origin} sent {quest_threat} crashing through {quest_focus}. Half our homes are underwater and {quest_stakes} have nowhere to go.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: We need someone to {quest_action} before the current carries it all away. {quest_item} have been scattered {location_hint} and we desperately need them. The {enemy_type} have been drawn to the chaos.
dialogueChunks.outro: People are cold, scared, and hungry. Every bit you recover could save a life.

playerResponses.text: I will wade in and pull out whatever I can.
Floodwaters and creatures? That is hazardous work.
There is nothing left to save down there.

dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, {quest_threat} is sweeping toward {quest_stakes} and nothing we have done has slowed it. {quest_focus} is ablaze and the wind is pushing it straight at us.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: We need to {quest_action} {quest_time_pressure}. Gather {quest_item} from {location_hint} to build firebreaks. Watch for {enemy_type}: the fire is driving them out of the woods and they are panicked.
dialogueChunks.outro: If that fire reaches the homesteads, we lose everything. Every moment counts.

playerResponses.text: People come first. I will get it done.
Fighting fire and beasts at the same time? Understood.
Running toward a wildfire is suicide.

dialogueChunks.intro: Since {quest_origin}, {player_name}, {quest_threat} keep coming. {quest_focus} are failing, and {quest_stakes} could collapse any day.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: We need to {quest_action} before the next tremor finishes what the first one started. Bring {quest_item} from {location_hint}. The {enemy_type} have been scavenging the rubble, so watch your back.
dialogueChunks.outro: The ground itself has turned against us. But we will not abandon our home without a fight.

playerResponses.text: Earthquakes and monsters. What a day. I will help.
Those walls look ready to fall on whoever is underneath. Dangerous.
Maybe it is time to move somewhere more stable.
